name: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch EnterpriseXG 24
title: Ubiquiti UniFi Switch EnterpriseXG 24
short_description: Enterprise XG24 Switch, (2) SFP28 Ports, (24) RJ45 10G Ports, 1.3'' touchscreen
description: Enterprise XG24 Switch, (2) SFP28 Ports, (24) RJ45 10G Ports, 1.3'' touchscreen

detailed_description.heading: High-Density Switching for Enterprise Networks
detailed_description.text: The UniFi Switch Enterprise 48 PoE is a Layer 3 enterprise-class switch designed for environments where high port density and extreme throughput are critical. It features twenty-four multi-speed 10 GbE RJ45 ports supporting 10G, 5G, 2.5G, 1G, and 100 Mbps connections, allowing seamless integration of modern high-performance devices alongside existing infrastructure. This makes the switch particularly well suited for Wi-Fi 6/6E access points, high-speed workstations, and bandwidth-intensive edge devices. For backbone connectivity and aggregation, the switch is equipped with two 25G SFP28 ports that support 25G, 10G, and 1G modules. These high-speed uplinks enable rapid data exchange between access, aggregation, and core layers, supporting scalable architectures and redundant network designs.

detailed_description.heading: Layer 3 and Layer 2 Capabilities
detailed_description.text: The UniFi Switch Enterprise 48 PoE delivers a comprehensive set of Layer 3 and Layer 2 features designed to meet the needs of complex enterprise environments. At Layer 3, the switch supports DHCP Server and DHCP Relay for local networks, enabling centralized IP address management. Inter-VLAN routing allows traffic to flow efficiently between network segments, while static routing provides predictable path control for internal traffic flows. Large routing and ARP tables ensure stable operation even in extensive, highly segmented deployments. At Layer 2, the switch offers a rich feature set focused on performance, security, and traffic optimization. LACP port aggregation enables higher bandwidth and link redundancy, while STP and RSTP protect the network from topology issues. Quality of Service with DSCP ensures that critical applications receive priority under load, which is especially important for voice, video, and real-time services. For professional audiovisual environments, built-in Pro AV profiles support popular protocols such as Dante, NDI, AES67, SDVoE, Q-SYS, and others. These profiles automatically optimize multicast behavior, traffic prioritization, and latency-sensitive parameters. Advanced multicast handling is implemented through IGMP Snooping and extended IGMP role configuration, ensuring efficient distribution of multicast streams without unnecessary network flooding. Security and control are enhanced through 802.1X authentication, MAC- and IP-based ACLs, DHCP snooping, device isolation, storm control, rate limiting, port mirroring, jumbo frames, LLDP-MED, Voice VLAN support, and loop protection.

detailed_description.heading: Reliable Power Options
detailed_description.text: The UniFi Switch Enterprise 48 PoE is designed for reliable operation in demanding enterprise environments. It supports a universal AC power input ranging from 100 to 240V at 50/60 Hz and includes an internal AC/DC power supply rated at 100W. For increased resilience, the switch also features a dedicated USP RPS DC input, enabling connection to an external redundant power system and reducing the risk of downtime caused by power failures. Despite its high-performance capabilities, the switch maintains efficient power consumption and controlled heat output, with a heat dissipation rating of 341.2 BTU/hr. This makes it suitable for dense rack installations where thermal management is critical. Centralized management is provided through the UniFi Network application (version 6.1.67 and later). The platform offers a single, unified interface for configuration, monitoring, analytics, and firmware updates across the entire UniFi ecosystem. Administrators can easily define network policies, monitor real-time traffic, troubleshoot issues, and manage large deployments from a centralized dashboard. Management access is available via Ethernet and AR, allowing both local and remote administration. A built-in 1.3-inch touchscreen display provides instant visibility into device status, port activity, and system alerts, simplifying on-site diagnostics and reducing maintenance time.

detailed_description.heading: Robust Construction
detailed_description.text: Housed in a durable SGCC steel enclosure, the UniFi Switch Enterprise 48 PoE is engineered for long-term reliability in professional installations. Its standard 1U rack-mount design enables seamless installation in 19-inch racks, making it well suited for data centers, server rooms, and enterprise network closets. Reinforced steel mounting hardware provides secure positioning and minimizes vibration during operation. The switch weighs 4.5 kg without mounting brackets and 4.6 kg with brackets, reflecting its solid industrial construction. The device is engineered for stable operation across a wide range of environmental conditions, supporting ambient temperatures from –5 to +40 °C and relative humidity levels between 10% and 95% without condensation, ensuring reliable performance in diverse deployment scenarios. Enhanced ESD protection (up to ±16 kV air discharge and ±12 kV contact discharge) helps protect internal components during installation and operation, especially in electrically noisy environments. The switch complies with NDAA requirements, making it suitable for use in regulated industries and government-related projects. It is certified to major international standards, including CE, FCC, IC, and Anatel, confirming compliance with safety, electromagnetic compatibility, and quality regulations across multiple regions. Together, these characteristics make the UniFi Switch Enterprise 48 PoE a dependable foundation for modern enterprise networks that demand performance, resilience, and regulatory compliance.
